data.title: Binance Introduces Sharia-Compliant Crypto Earning During Ramadan
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is launching Binance Sharia Earn, a Sharia-compliant crypto earning product, during Ramadan. This initiative aims to offer users a transparent and halal method to earn crypto rewards. The activity period is set from 2026-02-18 00:00 (UTC) to 2026-03-20 23:59 (UTC). During this time, eligible users can subscribe to BNB Locked Products under Sharia Earn for a chance to share 15 BNB equally. This opportunity allows users to earn in a manner that aligns with their financial goals and values.

To participate, users must log in to their verified Binance accounts and navigate to the Sharia Earn section under the Earn tab. They need to select eligible BNB Locked Products and subscribe for at least seven days during the activity period to qualify for the reward. Subscriptions made outside this period will not be eligible for the promotional APR. The reward structure ensures that eligible BNB subscriptions share 15 BNB equally, calculated and distributed in accordance with Sharia Earn product rules. After the activity period ends, subscriptions will continue under the standard APR applicable at that time.

Participation is subject to regional availability and product eligibility, with users required to opt in and subscribe during the activity period to qualify. Subscriptions may be subject to caps, availability, and lock-up terms as displayed on the product page. Rewards will be distributed to eligible users in token vouchers by 2026-04-20, with a validity period of 30 days from the day of distribution. Binance reserves the right to adjust APRs and amend activity rules at its discretion, ensuring compliance with the Binance Terms and Conditions for Prize Promotions.

data.title: Tesla Avoids Sales Suspension in California After Compliance with Marketing Practices
data.description: Tesla has successfully adhered to California's Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) regulations concerning the marketing of its vehicles' automated-driving features. Bloomberg posted on X, confirming that the company will not face a 30-day suspension of sales in the state. The compliance comes after scrutiny over how Tesla promoted its self-driving technology, ensuring alignment with state guidelines. This development allows Tesla to continue its operations in California without interruption, maintaining its presence in one of its key markets.

data.title: YouTube Experiences Widespread Outage in the U.S.
data.description: YouTube users across the United States reported significant service disruptions, according to DownDetector. The monitoring site indicated that approximately 240,707 users experienced issues accessing the platform. According to Jin10, the outage affected a large number of users, causing inconvenience and prompting widespread attention. The cause of the disruption has not been disclosed, and users are awaiting updates from YouTube regarding the resolution of the issue.

data.title: Australia's Wage Growth Poses Inflation Challenge for Reserve Bank
data.description: Australia's annual wage growth remained high in the last quarter, highlighting the inflationary pressures confronting the Reserve Bank of Australia (RBA). Bloomberg posted on X, noting that this development follows the central bank's recent decision to raise interest rates. The persistent wage growth suggests that inflationary challenges may continue, potentially influencing future monetary policy decisions by the RBA. The central bank's efforts to manage inflation are critical as it seeks to balance economic growth with price stability.

data.title: New Zealand Central Bank Maintains Interest Rate at 2.25%
data.description: The Reserve Bank of New Zealand has decided to keep its interest rate steady at 2.25%, aligning with market expectations. According to Jin10, this decision marks the end of a series of rate cuts over the past three meetings. The central bank's move reflects a cautious approach amid ongoing economic assessments.

data.title: Ethiopia's Electric Vehicle Adoption Surpasses Global Average
data.description: Ethiopia is experiencing a significant rise in electric vehicle (EV) adoption, outpacing the global average. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this surge is largely attributed to the country's impending 2024 ban on the importation of fossil fuel-powered vehicles. This policy shift is part of Ethiopia's broader strategy to reduce carbon emissions and promote sustainable energy solutions. The move is expected to transform the nation's automotive landscape, encouraging both consumers and manufacturers to pivot towards electric alternatives. As the deadline approaches, Ethiopia's commitment to environmental sustainability is drawing attention on the international stage.

data.title: BTC Treasury Companies Face Criticism for Shareholder Practices
data.description: Benjamin Cowen, founder and CEO of Into The Cryptoverse, posted on X that while not all Bitcoin treasury companies are problematic, a significant number engage in practices aimed at extracting maximum value from their shareholders. This behavior mirrors the actions of many altcoins that these companies have previously criticized. The critique highlights ongoing concerns within the cryptocurrency community regarding the ethical practices of some firms managing Bitcoin treasuries.

data.title: BOJ to tighten as IMF flags two 2025 hikes, one 2026
data.description:
IMF forecast for BOJ rates, BOJ interest rate hikes 2025, Japan neutral interest rate underpins gradual tightening; data point to durable inflation and wages.
source: https://coincu.com/news/boj-to-tighten-as-imf-flags-two-2025-hikes-one-2026/

data.title: The WLFI project team withdrew 313 million WLFI tokens from Binance, worth $33.76 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, in the past 11 hours, the WLFI project address (0xd1fc...f6ee) withdrew 313 million WLFI tokens from Binance, worth $33.76 million.

data.title: Hungary's Upcoming Election Faces Uncertainty Amid U.S. Support for Orban
data.description:
Hungary is set to hold elections in April, with polls indicating a challenging outlook for the ruling party led by current Prime Minister Viktor Orban. According to RTHK, U.S. Secretary of State Rubio recently visited Hungary and met with Orban, reaffirming U.S. President Donald Trump's full support for Orban, stating that his continued leadership aligns with American interests.

Orban is currently serving his fifth term as Prime Minister, making him the longest-serving leader in Hungary's history. In recent years, he has taken a pro-Russian stance on the Russia-Ukraine issue, which contrasts with the European Union's position, while maintaining close relations with the current U.S. administration. As tensions between Europe and the United States have recently increased, observers are watching to see if the situation in Hungary will influence the regional political landscape.

data.title: Pension-usdt.eth Deposits $30 Million USDC into Hyperliquid for Leveraged Bitcoin Position
data.description: The on-chain entity pension-usdt.eth has made a significant financial move by depositing an additional $30 million USDC into the Hyperliquid platform. According to NS3.AI, this deposit was used to open a 3x leveraged long position on 1000 BTC, valued at $67.59 million. The position has an entry price of $67,203.6, with a liquidation price set at $37,681.1.

data.title: Former Circle Executive Joins Hyperliquid Labs to Lead Business Development
data.description:
Sterling, the former head of business development at Circle, announced on the X platform that he has joined Hyperliquid Labs. According to ChainCatcher, Sterling will be responsible for business expansion and establishing partnerships.

His initial focus will be on collaborating with developers worldwide to expand Hyperliquid's builder code integration ecosystem.

data.title: Analytics Tools Revolutionize Trading Desks
data.description: Analytics tools are significantly transforming trading desks by facilitating evidence-based decision-making and uncovering the true drivers of performance. FactSet posted on X, highlighting the importance of confidence, context, and delivery in making informed trading decisions. These tools provide traders with the necessary insights to enhance their strategies and improve outcomes. By leveraging data analytics, trading desks can better understand market dynamics and make more informed choices, ultimately leading to improved performance and efficiency in trading operations.

data.title: Coinbase CEO responds to being underestimated by Wall Street: A classic "innovator's dilemma," with traditional finance showing a clear divergence in attitudes toward crypto.
data.description: Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong responded to "Why Wall Street Always Misunderstands or Underestimates Coinbase" in an article on the X platform. He stated that traditional financial institutions are showing a clear divergence in their attitudes towards the crypto industry. Some leading financial institutions are actively embracing crypto assets; five Global Systemically Important Banks (GSIBs) have already begun collaborating with Coinbase, and about half of the major financial institutions are accelerating their crypto business development and recruiting related talent. As regulatory clarity gradually increases, traditional finance's acceptance of the crypto industry is continuously improving. However, at the same time, some institutions remain hesitant or even resistant. These institutions' career paths have long been built on the traditional financial system, so they are naturally skeptical of the structural impact of crypto. Similar to the taxi industry facing Uber, the hotel industry facing Airbnb, and the aerospace industry facing SpaceX, cryptocurrencies are directly disrupting Wall Street, so it's not surprising that some people on Wall Street misunderstand cryptocurrencies/Coinbase. In addition, Brian Armstrong disclosed Coinbase's performance data, including a 156% year-on-year increase in total transaction volume and a doubling of market share in 2025, a three-fold increase in the scale of assets under custody on the platform in three years, and 12 products with annualized revenue exceeding $100 million. Investors should focus on the company's long-term execution capabilities and business performance record, rather than simply relying on analyst model predictions. Furthermore, since GAAP net profit includes unrealized gains and losses from the company's holdings of crypto assets, adjusted net profit better reflects the true operating situation.

data.title: K3 Capital-related wallets purchased 20,000 ETH from Binance, worth $40.08 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet associated with K3 Capital (0x4352...3b4f) purchased 20,000 ETH from Binance, worth $40.08 million.

data.title: John Paulson Divests Stake in Trilogy Metals After Over Ten Years
data.description: Billionaire hedge-fund manager John Paulson has sold his stake in Trilogy Metals, marking the end of his investment in the company after more than a decade. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this significant move in the financial markets. Paulson's decision to divest comes as part of a broader strategy shift, although specific reasons for the sale were not disclosed. Trilogy Metals, a company focused on exploring and developing mineral resources, has been a part of Paulson's investment portfolio for several years. The sale of his stake could have implications for the company's future direction and investor confidence. Paulson's investment strategies have often been closely watched by market analysts, given his reputation for making bold and successful financial moves. This latest development is expected to attract attention from both investors and industry observers, as it may signal potential changes in the market dynamics surrounding Trilogy Metals.

data.title: Antitrust Trial Against Live Nation to Proceed Despite Potential DOJ Settlement
data.description: Several state attorneys general, including those from California and Connecticut, are set to continue with an antitrust trial aimed at breaking up Live Nation. Bloomberg posted on X that this move will proceed even if the U.S. Justice Department opts to settle the case. The trial reflects ongoing concerns about Live Nation's market dominance and its impact on competition within the entertainment industry. The attorneys general are determined to address these issues through legal proceedings, highlighting the significance of maintaining competitive practices in the market.

data.title: Angolan Journalist Targeted by Government-Grade Spyware
data.description: A prominent journalist in Angola has been targeted by government-grade spyware, marking the first known instance of such an occurrence in the southern African nation. Bloomberg posted on X, revealing that researchers have identified this case as a significant development in the region. The use of advanced surveillance technology against journalists raises concerns about press freedom and privacy in Angola. This incident highlights the growing trend of employing sophisticated spyware to monitor and potentially suppress journalistic activities. The implications of this discovery are likely to resonate beyond Angola, as it underscores the challenges faced by journalists in maintaining their independence and safety in the digital age.

data.title: Reform UK Proposes Changes to Bank of England and Budget Watchdog
data.description: Reform UK has announced plans to restructure the Bank of England and the government's budget watchdog if it secures victory in the upcoming general election. Bloomberg posted on X that Robert Jenrick, the party's newly appointed Treasury spokesman, is expected to outline these proposals on Wednesday. The party aims to implement significant changes to the financial institutions to enhance their efficiency and accountability. Reform UK's strategy reflects its commitment to revamping the country's economic framework, focusing on transparency and improved governance. The proposed reforms are part of a broader agenda to address economic challenges and ensure sustainable growth. The party believes that these changes will better align the institutions with the nation's financial goals and priorities.

data.title: Japan's Export Growth Accelerates Amid Increased Shipments to China and EU
data.description:
Japan's exports have experienced their most rapid growth in over three years, driven by a surge in shipments to China and the European Union. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant increase in trade activity. This development marks a notable recovery for Japan's economy, which has been striving to rebound from previous challenges.

The rise in exports is attributed to strong demand from China and the EU, two of Japan's key trading partners. The increased shipments reflect a broader trend of economic recovery and growing international trade relations. Analysts suggest that this growth could have positive implications for Japan's economic outlook, potentially boosting its GDP and strengthening its position in global markets.

Japan's export sector has been a critical component of its economy, and the recent surge underscores the importance of international trade in driving economic growth. As global demand continues to rise, Japan's exporters are poised to benefit from increased opportunities in foreign markets.

The latest data on export growth comes amid ongoing efforts by Japan to enhance its trade partnerships and expand its economic influence. The country's focus on strengthening ties with China and the EU is seen as a strategic move to capitalize on emerging market trends and secure long-term economic stability.

Overall, the acceleration in Japan's exports signals a positive trajectory for the nation's economy, with potential benefits extending to various sectors and industries. As Japan continues to navigate the complexities of global trade, its export performance will remain a key indicator of economic health and resilience.
